This biographical article relating to Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Khizra_Rasheed&action=edit).

(born 24 February 1988) is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1) She was the bronze medalist at the [2016 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2016_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2016 South Asian Games") in the women's doubles event,[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) and the champion at the 2017 [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

["Players: Khizra Rasheed"](http://bwfbadminton.com/player/97224/khizra-rasheed). *bwfbadminton.com*. [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation"). Retrieved 12 November 2016.

["XII South Asian Games"](http://www.sports.gov.pk/Participation/12th_SA_Games.html). *www.sports.gov.pk*. [Pakistan Sports Board](/wiki/Pakistan_Sports_Board "Pakistan Sports Board"). Retrieved 16 December 2019.

["South Asian Games : Ruthvika Shivani Upset PV Sindhu To Win Gold"](http://xtratime.in/south-asian-games-ruthvika-shivani-upset-pv-sindhu-to-win-gold/). *xtratime.in*. 10 February 2016. Retrieved 16 December 2019.

["Pakistan nail three of five titles in Yonex Sunrise Pakistan International Series"](https://www.badmintonasia.org/updates/news/pakistan-nail-three-of-five-titles-in-yonex-sunrise-pakistan-international-series?year=2017). *www.badmintonasia.org*. [Badminton Asia](/wiki/Badminton_Asia "Badminton Asia"). 13 November 2017. Retrieved 18 December 2019.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani badminton player (born 1988)

Khizra Rasheed

**Khizra Rasheed** (born 24 February 1988) is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1) She was the bronze medalist at the [2016 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2016_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2016 South Asian Games") in the women's doubles event,[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) and the champion at the 2017 [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
